Operation 'rectifyZone' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable. Original summary is too generic and doesn't specify DNSSEC context. Description is incomplete and reads more like a limitation list than an operation explanation. Improved version clarifies: (1) the specific action (DNSSEC signature rectification), (2) prerequisites (DNSSEC-enabled, authoritative zone), (3) what is returned (Zone object), (4) side effects (signature modification, serial update), and (5) maintains the important limitation about API-RECTIFY metadata. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_summary: Rectify the zone data.
data.current_description: This does not take into account the API-RECTIFY metadata. Fails on slave zones and zones that do not have DNSSEC.
data.summary_suggestion: Rectify DNSSEC signatures for authoritative zone
data.description_suggestion: Rectifies DNSSEC signatures for an authoritative DNS zone by recalculating and updating RRSIG records to match the current zone content. This operation requires the zone to be DNSSEC-enabled and authoritative (not a secondary/slave zone). Note: This operation does not consider the API-RECTIFY metadata setting. Returns the updated Zone object with rectified DNSSEC signatures. Side effects: Modifies zone signatures and updates the zone's serial number.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and doesn't specify DNSSEC context. Description is incomplete and reads more like a limitation list than an operation explanation. Improved version clarifies: (1) the specific action (DNSSEC signature rectification), (2) prerequisites (DNSSEC-enabled, authoritative zone), (3) what is returned (Zone object), (4) side effects (signature modification, serial update), and (5) maintains the important limitation about API-RECTIFY metadata. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_summary: Returns the zone in AXFR format.
data.description_suggestion: Exports the specified zone in AXFR (full zone transfer) format. This operation retrieves the complete zone data including all resource records in a format suitable for zone transfers. Returns the zone data as a text response in standard AXFR format. Requires the zone to exist on the specified server.
data.reasoning: Original summary is adequate but description is completely missing. Added context about AXFR purpose, what data is returned, response format, and prerequisites for AI agent understanding.

data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is missing entirely. Added details about what gets deleted, parameters, return value, and side effects (permanence) for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Get the content of a single kind of domain metadata as a Metadata object.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves metadata entries of a specific kind from the zone. Returns a Metadata object containing all entries of the specified metadata_kind. If no metadata of that kind exists for the zone, returns an empty Metadata object.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but verbose. Description is missing entirely. Added details about return value structure and edge case behavior (empty result) for better AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'notifyZone' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is clear and action-oriented. data.current_summary: Send a DNS NOTIFY to all slaves.
data.current_description: Fails when zone kind is not Master or Slave, or master and slave are disabled in the configuration. Only works for Slave if renotify is on. Clients MUST NOT send a body.
data.description_suggestion: Sends a DNS NOTIFY message to all slave servers for the specified zone. This operation triggers zone transfer notifications to secondary nameservers. Only works for zones with kind Master or Slave (when renotify is enabled). Fails if the zone kind is not Master/Slave or if master/slave functionality is disabled in configuration. Request body must be empty. Returns success confirmation when NOTIFY messages are sent.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description covers prerequisites and constraints but lacks clarity on return value and success criteria. Reorganized description for better flow and added return value information for AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Get all TSIGKeys on the server, except the actual key
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all TSIG keys configured on the server. Returns an array of TSIGKey objects with metadata (name, algorithm, created date) but excludes the actual key material for security reasons. TSIG keys are used to authenticate NOTIFY, AXFR, and DNSUPDATE queries.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is missing. Added explanation of what is returned, why the actual key is excluded (security), and the purpose of TSIG keys in the PowerDNS context to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Add a TSIG key
data.current_description: This methods add a new TSIGKey. The actual key can be generated by the server or be provided by the client
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new TSIG key on the server. The key material can be auto-generated by the server or provided by the client in the request body. Returns the created TSIGKey object (201 Created). The new key can immediately be used to authenticate NOTIFY, AXFR, and DNSUPDATE queries.

data.current_summary: Delete the TSIGKey with tsigkey_id

data.current_summary: Retrieve slave zone from its master.
data.current_description: Fails when zone kind is not Slave, or slave is disabled in the configuration. Clients MUST NOT send a body.
data.summary_suggestion: Trigger AXFR retrieval from master server
data.description_suggestion: Initiates an AXFR (zone transfer) retrieval from the zone's master server. Only applicable to zones with kind=Slave. Fails if zone kind is not Slave or if slave zone transfers are disabled in configuration. Clients MUST NOT send a request body. Returns the updated zone data upon successful transfer.
data.reasoning: Original summary uses 'slave' terminology which is outdated in DNS context. Description lacks clarity about what AXFR means, when it applies, and what is returned. Enhanced version adds context about zone transfer mechanism, explicit applicability conditions, and return value for better AI agent understanding.
